destinations.evidence: At capacity, registration attempts remove only inactive executors and choose the oldest inactive registration_time before inserting the new executor.

equivalence_argument: ExecutorRegistry::register_executor invokes capacity handling before insertion, and the helper targets the inactive entry with the minimum registration_time.
rationale: The registration path invokes capacity handling and the helper chooses the minimum registration_time among inactive entries, preserving newer inactive records and all active records when possible. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When registering an executor while the registry has reached max_executors, the registry attempts to make room by removing the inactive executor with the oldest registration_time before inserting the new entry.
suggested_falsification_tests: Create a registry at capacity with multiple inactive executors whose registration_time values differ, register a new executor, and assert the oldest inactive address was removed while newer inactive and active entries remain.
summary: Capacity eviction targets the oldest inactive executor.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Fill a registry to capacity with multiple inactive executors of different registration_time values and one active executor, then register a new executor and assert the oldest inactive one was removed.
title: Capacity eviction targets oldest inactive executor
